10.response: {"Q0":"No, everything was made clear. The instructions were very well done."}

6.response: {"Q0":"No I did not. Clicking on a gem in the meteor shower cost 10 gold. Collecting gems  that we can no longer sell, therefore, does not make sense financially."}

5.response: {"Q0":"Absolutely not! Doing so would have meant less money!"}

11.response: {"Q0":"I am guessing it has to do with how people balance risk vs reward under pressure (in this case, a time limit). "}

0.response: {"Q0":"I memorized the order scheme for each planet to start. Once you recognize the pattern, it makes it much easier. I also made sure to choose a galaxy on the first showing versus the last; that way when it popped up for the second time, I was already looking at the appropriate galaxy, and simply had to hit the appropriate button. "}

7.response: {"Q0":"When both astronauts were present, I would happily collect the green ice. However, once one left, I no longer collected the green ice as there is no way to tell if it contains the right gem or not."}

9.response: {"Q0":"I felt my attention was spread pretty evenly throughout the task. I realized each part would impact my payout, so I tried to really focus on every aspect."}

1.response: {"Q0":"My strategy essentially stayed the same. As I learned new information, such as when the red astronaut flew away, I adjusted my strategy accordingly."}

2.response: {"Q0":"It seemed to be a pretty even split. I would guess it was very close to being 50-50."}

8.response: {"Q0":"I tended to go with the galaxy that had a higher chance of success. There were some exceptions; if the percentages were close, I would sometimes choose the higher number even if it had a lesser success rate."}

3.response: {"Q0":"Yes, I always noticed and took note accordingly."}

4.response: {"Q0":"Yes, the implications were made very clear! "}

12.response: {"Q0":"I did not encounter any bugs throughout the game."}
